title: 2017 · In-season — The 'Thameslink' trade-dispute rant
text:
*Sent 24/10/2017*

My fellow thameslinkers....we have a trade in our midst that smells of something rotten. As can be seen above we had agreed that trades and all that goes with them will be put on the group in order to ensure the integrity of the league remains intact. This has not been done in the trade in question and since being picked up by jamie kay earlier this evening, both parties have had the opportunity to reply and discuss why it is a worthwhile trade for both. Given the lack of transparency and therefore fear of potential collusion and given that both parties have had ample opportunity to provide evidence for the trade and have both not come forward with any sort of solid justification.....I move to veto this trade....the rules created by the commissioner require 5 people to veto....i shall be the first to say I am using my right to veto this trade...

title: 2017 · REGULAR-SEASON REVIEW / Playoff opener
text:
*Sent 05/12/2017*

Here we are then it is week 14 and the start of the business end of the journey to the Plancey bowl. The regular season ended in spectacular fashion seeing the sort of magic and excitement “survival Sunday” would be proud of. After some incredible collapses from middle of the season favourite Josh Gaon (losing all of his 6 second round games) and Daniel Polak (losing 5 of his 6 second round games) it created the opportunity for those who may have believed their season was over to climb up the league and into playoff bliss. *Jamie Kay*, had the first pick in the draft only to see his 30 point a week shoe in David Johnson go down with a season ending injury in the first week. Nobody would have blamed him for giving up, however through perseverance and strong waiver pickups (and a few more season ending injuries) he managed to pull himself back in to playoff contention and on to a 5-7 record going  in to the final week of the regular season, only to pull off one of the performances of the season when it really mattered. On paper last seasons regular season champion may not have the strongest team, but he has built a side of secondary players stepping up to the plate after the main starters have become injured or left. Therefore don't rule out special K just yet. 
Now what may be the story of the regular season. *Not if but wentz* were the team to play in the first round of games in the regular season. The reigning champion had a mixture of season damaging injuries and crippling bad luck that saw him rooted to the bottom of the league. Despite scoring in the higher echelons of the league in terms of points it seemed everyone turned up when playing the champ. However, as the weeks came and went amd using his superior nfl knowledge and his consistent waiver priority to pick up one of the strongest line ups heading in to the playoffs. 
Now 3 weeks ago *Sacofsky* was planning for his extended vacation and time with the family with no hope of playoff action for his boys. However, in this league 3 wins on the bounce will take you places and so it did. 3 massive performances, thanks to a couple of strong trades and his boys coming good at the right time saw Sac book his place in the playoffs. The last and most important win against Josh Gaon going to the final quarter of monday night football after some deadline day trading activity. Sac has been hurt right at the end of his match ups before, so we have no doubt he was sweating at the end of this one. With Wilson, Brown and Kamara on fire expect to see Sac meandering his way through the playoffs to a possile plancey bowl appearance.
On draft day, if we were making our predicitions over which player debutant was likely to make the playoffs in his first season there would have only been one answer. Josh "loves a trade" Gaon had brought sheets upon sheets of research to his first Borehamwood draft. *Lev*, on the other hand, could not even muster up the 2 minutes it takes to register his team, leaving it to the league commissioner to do. However, in true Lev fashion, its not about getting there as long as you reach the required destination. Lev has quietly gone about his business jumping on the back of a strong season from Dak only to have stayed on the ride a bit too long. However, big nights from Jones and Hopkins give Lev's team the ability to go big on any given night....just ask Gaon. A strong first season by any standards, anything from here would be magic. 

The bye week lads


The regular season belonged to one team and one team only. The *shprutzrangers* have had an absolute stormer of a season the likes of which have not been since since last season with Jamie Kay. Zeke, Ingram and Smith have made this team almost impossible to beat during the regular season. However, some dodgy trading decisions have meant that the team is a little more depleted going in to the playoffs, however only a fool would not give them a chance to go all the way. As Mr Kay would attest, the regular season means nothing if you cant go all the way and some slightly poorer performances in the lead up to the playoffs lead people to wonder if he can claim the coveted belt. The stats dont lie...he rarely loses and he rarely goes under 100 points....Write him off at your peril. 
They often say that over time dogs and owners begin to look alike. This couldnt be more true for the second of our bye week teams. *Saul* has created a team in his image, he has pushed them to be the leagues most efficient outfit playing the match up and not getting ahead of themselves. Scathed throughout the league much like a Mourinho team, Freedman’s style of play has been criticised and admired throughout the league. Saul looks to be saving his best scores for when it really matters as he took advantage of a desperate Josh Gaon to secure what may be the trade of the season. We make leavemyarsalona favourites for the Plancey without blowout victories...however knowing saul he will probably do just enough.

---

title: 2017 · Round-1 preview — Levey vs Sac
text:
*Sent 07/12/2017*

*Levey vs Sac*
Its kept in the family in the first of our matchups as the Scrumpy Sac face off against the cleverly named....Levey.
Levey will have much to prove against a resurgent Sacofsky as he is the team in the playoffs with the lowest regular season record. As first cousins it was a beautiful thing to watch as the Sac victory meant that by virtue of the mini league Lev would secure his place in the playoffs at the expense of Gaon and Polak. We have been told Stephen, Daniella, Charlie and Karen are having a metsuyan dinner to celebrate this act of family love. But family ties go out the window come thursday night as each will seek to continue their playoff journey. 

Based on recent form, on paper there looks to be only one winner as Sac looks to continue his hot winning streak that saw him win his last 3 games and sail in to the playoffs as the 3rd best team. If Lev wants to make his mark he will most likely have to rely on a big night from one of his studs. 
A first season and a first playoff appearance for Levey has left the fans, who have coined themselves “The Levites”, in absolute delight. All wasn’t well at the start of the season as we reported on the fans bewilderment with the new boss at the helm given the teams acquisition by Benjy Levey. We interviewed one fan who asked to remain anonymous, “I just can’t understand the acquisition it seems like he doesn’t understand football and is only in it for the money from the Plancey name for his catering business”.
However, Levey has really grown in to the role, after making the shock playoff berth another fan stated, “It is just unbelievable, this has made my year. I didn’t expect it and the boys down the local definitely were not expecting it. It is not easy supporting a team like Levey, at times it seems like the boss doesn’t care at all. I live in the USA so I set my alarm for 4am every Wednesday morning to check the transfers we have made and to my horror the boss misses his spot on the waiver wire time and time again! A lot of the fans started to boo the lads off the pitch when things were at their worst. But the board backed him and in Lev we trust. It was sometimes nervy, having 5 inactive players in the starting line up with 5 minutes to go until kick off, but he always seems to get there in the end, COYL!”. 
Coming under fire for a possible lack of focus on the team, Levey told the fans to “Trust the Process” and it seems it has worked out, but the real work starts now. 
Levey has had some serious QB issues recently with Dak being so dependable for so long only to have fallen off in recent weeks, Goff was picked up on the waiver but he has a tough matchup this week vs the eagles. Therefore expect Dak to come back in against a poor Giants. From Sacs side of things Russell Wilson has grown in to the season massively to become the no.1 ranked QB in fantasy. He has a tough matchup vs Jax but when you are Russell Wilson the matchup can sometimes be irrelevant. 
From a Wide Recievers standpoint this is where Levey can take the game by the scuff of the neck, his two stars in Hopkins and Jones both provide massive opportunities to take the game away from any fantasy team out there. Its the inconsistencies and lack of forn that may provide Levs undoing in this one. Against Sac you will need your WR’s to perform just to keep pace with the back in form Antonio Brown. If he continues his strong fantasy scores of late that could be lights out. 
From a RB perspective there will only be one winner on paper. The Scrumpy line up of Howard and Kamara (a team made in Gaon heaven) with good matchups are going to be very difficult to beat. However, Lev has great matchups for his boys with Miller playing the 49ers and Lewis facing Miami therefore it isn’t a complete foregone conclusion. Watch this space. 
From a TE and Kicker perspective, lets be honest if you don’t have the best…who the fuck knows what going to happen, its Cook/seferian Jenkins vs Walker/Henry and having owned the majority of these guys at some point in the season, it could be 1 point or it could be 10, I refuse to predicting anything. Lutz vs Forbath seems a little bit of a consistency vs potentially large score. Lutz has been one of the most consistent kickers this year always around the 8 point mark, whereas Forbath has hit 22 points at one point this season and yet last week he scored 2. Very tough to call. 
From a defensive standpoint the Ravens are higher ranked than Denver but facing Pittsburgh looks to me to be a bit of a difficult one although, Big Ben loves his interceptions. 
All in all, I think Sac on current form should take this one to then go and face this seasons Plancey favourite Leavemyarsalona in the semi Planceys. There is already some bad blood there with Leavemyarsalona only slightly beating Sac over both games in the regular season. On the other hand Lev knows his boys need to do so much better than last week, he will be hoping his big boys perform to give the Levites another week in the sun. The stage is set for the two cousins to go to battle…..

---

title: 2017 · Round-1 preview — Not if but Wentz vs Special K
text:
*Sent 07/12/2017*

*Not if but Wentz vs Special K* 
Last years Plancey bowlers will reignite their feud this week to secure a place against the Shprutzrangers….For Jamie Kay, he will be looking to exact some revenge….
Not if but Wentz fans were starting to wonder what went wrong at the beginning of the season, “I have been an avid fan for the last 1 year and 3 months and I wanted to support a winning team. Livingston had taken us to glory last year and received a lifelong contract on the back of that and I think it got to his head. We started the season with high hopes, Livingston had told us that he had the “perfect” draft. But we just kept losing. I had to pull sickies at work on Tuesdays, everyone was just taking the piss it was unbearable. But Livingston has certainly pulled his finger out and got us back to playoffs where we belong”. 
Livingston will be looking to get himself a semi Plancey place against regular season champs the Shprutrangers. But standing in his way is a resolute Jamie Kay on the back of a 150+ point week in a clutch performance for the ages. He will be looking for some revenge for last years Plancey bowl. Kay lost his First round draft pick early on in the season and had spent the rest of the season in a pessimistic lull, however, a late flurry has turned Kay and the fans back in to believers, they are so excited to be back in the playoffs. We got in touch with David Johnson, “I was so upset to pick up an injury so early on in the season for the gaffer. I called him up in tears to tell him the news. We sat up all night crying and trying to work out a way we could make it work. He offered to put his medical career on the line to give me a doctors note to say I was fit to suit up but I couldn’t do that to him. I was so glad he allowed me to stick around with the team in a coaching role to encourage the boys to keep believing and keep going. I was so excited to see the lads clutch performance. I will be there this weekend at JK’s side if he needs me for anything. We can do this!”
In the QB positions Special K have had problems for quite some time due to injuries meaning that streaming based on matchup has been the only option. Currently on the roster we have Rivers and the recent pickup of McCowan. It is hard to say which of the two Kay is going to go ahead with, it seems McCowan vs Denver is the better matchup this week aswell as being in good form. If picked, Kay will be hoping for another 30+ week. Livingston has his pick of either Cam or Cousins. Cousins my have the better match up but as seen this season Cam can go big on any given night. The fans await a decision from both managers on the QB position, keep refreshing for any last minute changes. 
From a running backs perspective it may not look pretty but don’t count Kay out of this one. Drake, Williams and Burkhead whilst not household names have strong match ups and a lot of touches. Fournette and Gordon have performed better, but given the match-up it is actually a tough one to call based on recent weeks. 
From a WR perspective Fitzgerald and Thielin have strong matchups, with Thielin expecting a big week this week at the expense of Diggs…again. Cooks, Anderson and Jeffery provide solid options for Not if but wentz, in strong match ups. Therefore this will also be a hard one to call. 
From a TE perspective Kelce had a monster start last week which was the trigger for a massive week for Special K and he will be hoping for the same again. As studs go Kelce is the one for Special K and he will be hoping for a strong performance to see him through. Engram has also had a strong year ranking in at 5th overall. He had a big week last week and Livingston will be hoping for the same again against a poor Dallas side. 
If you are in to your kickers this week is the match up for you, we have the 1st and 2nd ranked kickers in this one Greg the leg vs Gostkowski. Expect big numbers from these two regardless of matchup. Greg has been a kicker performing like a top 10 running back this season and constantly hits double figures. But Gostkowski vs Miami turns this in to a real Kickers matchup like which we may have never seen. 
Seattle vs the Steelers in Defence is quite a tough one to call both strong and both have big number abilities. The Legion are depleted but still managed to post good numbers vs eagles so I am going to go with them in this matchup. 
Overall, this could turn in to a very close contest, Kay has built himself a strong team through the waiver wire and is taking it week by week, he will need a week like last to get through I feel. Livingston has got his team back on the winning trail when all looked lost at the beginning of the season. I put Special K as slight underdogs for this one…The Shprutzrangers await….

---

title: 2017 · Semi-Plancey preview (part 1)
text:
*Sent 14/12/2017*

We have had last quarter wins, we have had the dramas of the final week and we have had some stonker game weeks.....and then came week 14.....the biggest news being that the champion has fallen!
It would not have been outrageous at week 7 or even week 10 to say that, by all accounts, the favourites to miss out on the playoffs were special K and Levey. The respective owners may not have disagreed with you...but here we are at the final four and both will most certainly have eyes on a achieving the unthinkable. We are at the stage where pretending your not good enough to go all the way is no longer a viable option. The stakes are high but the reward is nothing short of incredible glory. 
*Special K vs The Shprutzrangers*
Jamie kay may believe he was robbed of what looked like a certain Plancey win last year and therefore, revenge could not have been sweeter as his team performed for him once again to take one step closer to redemption. He will now look to slay the mighty shprutzrangers and after defeating Livingston in the final round, who would bet against them now! 
I kid you not, through the first 8 weeks of the season Williams combined for a total score of just over 5 points. Given injuries and thus a move up in the pecking order, he has been nothing short of an absolute beast since. He could be seen as one of the pickups of the season and is testament to the work Kay has been doing at the franchise. There has been alot of investment in the scouting set up and the fans have really bought in to it, “if you had told me that our team would have drake and williams as RB1 and 2 going in to the playoffs I would have laughed you out the building, my days has the boss come through for us”. Now nicknamed “The medicine” for taking a team packed with injuries to their second semi plancey in 2 years, Kay will be looking to make it a second Plancey bowl appearance in 2 years against sanford. 

Benjy Sanford has had quite a year. He is looking to achieve the incredible feat of Miriam to Plancey in the space of just one year. The Shprutzrangers made short work of the regular season coming in to the playoffs on the back of a 10-3 win loss record. But being regular season champ and owning the Plancey belt are two different matters altogether. 

On the back of massive years for Zeke and Smith along with the emergence of the Jaguars D, the shrutzrangers have been formidable. We got in touch with a fan, "After last season, I am not sure how sanford didnt get the sack. It seems he was more interested in Tinder and Jswipe than putting out a decent team last year. However, i heard the gaffer got engaged in preseason and you can see its really given him a renewed focus. We have gone from no-hopers to champions elect in no time". 

They say to win a Plancey it takes nerves of steel. The fans were up in arms mid season where maybe a little inexperience showed in a trade with Josh Gaon. Giving up Zeke mid season made sense and so it proved, however giving up Howard was another thing altogether. We caught up with Howard, "we had a beautiful thing going, I was playing well in a winning team. We all knew the boss was looking to offload zeke given the suspension. I was in prime position to become the teams RB1. I had to pull over when i got the call i was on the move, to Gaon of all players. I was told by other players that the move meant I was probably on the move again quite quickly and so it proved. My performances have been effected and Sanford has turned me in to a league journeyman".
Interestingly, in the lead up to the Semi Plancey there have been rumblings in the Shprutzrangers camp, a team that has been so used to hitting over 100 points have come in to the playoffs on the back of some below average performances. There is no doubt there has been a slight drop in form, however, in his week 15 press conference, Sanford looked to calm the Shprutzranger fans, “We are the regular season champs, we fear nobody going in the playoffs and we will come good when the pressure is on us again”. There has been news of internal issues within the team with a source saying, “The player have become tense as the season has dragged on. They know they are the best team, but they are scared of what Sanford will do next. It seems Sanfords trading reputation has left even the best players in the team worried about their place and you can see it in their recent performances”. 
We have been told Sanford was extremely upset that he couldn’t get through a big trade before the trading deadline with Gaon. The reports have stated Sanford was offering Smith, Ingram, Hill and the Jacksonville Defence for Ricky Seals-Jones. Supposedly Sanford pulled the plug in the 11th hour in the view that he had no more studs to offer to get his man. 
This is a match-up of epic proportions, where a team very used to a playoff run go up against a team playing their first ever playoff game. Who will hold their nerve….

To the match preview…. 
QB’s- Smith vs Rivers – We have a QB shootout in this one as KC face LAC. Kay picked up Rivers from a QB heavy Livingston prior to the playoffs, only to have him slay his old owner in Week 14. Kay will be hoping for a continued run this week. Sanford has proven slightly more cautious of his early season MVP Smith. We expect Sanford to put his faith in his man to get him to his first Plancey given the possibility of huge numbers if the day is right. 
RB’s- Sanford used to have a plethora of talent in this position, this week he looks to be playing the matchups and the form guide as Riddick is picked up off the waiver wire and straight in to the first team….Sanford has had immense good fortune with these picks leading to some questions of having friends on the inside. Riddick has performed strongly in recent weeks and Sanford expects this to continue. Ingram is first name on the teamsheet, despite sharing with Kamara, he still puts up strong numbers for Sanford and against the Jets he expects to do the same. 
Kay will stick to his dream team of Drake and Williams as his RB1 and RB2, they got him this far, why change it now. Both have been putting up MVP level numbers in recent weeks and if they do it again, expect to see another preview for Jamie Kay next week. Kay currently has Collins in the RB3 position and who can blame him when he is up against Cleveland. Kay knows how to play the matchups and it has worked in his favour, he won’t stop now as he smells blood. 
WR’s – Sanford keeps his faith in Landry and Hill who have served him well throughout the season. Landry is on the back of a 20 point game and will be looking to do the job for his gaffer against Buffalo. Funchess has been posting double figures for quite some time now, we expect this to continue vs GB. Hill will be looking to give Sanford a double point bonus by connecting with Smith this week. The matchup vs the Chargers give them an opportunity, it just depends what KC turn up. Sanford will also be hoping the ball is thrown Hills way as opposed to one Travis Kelce. 
Kay will once again stick with Fitzgerald and Thielin. Thielin continues to put up big numbers as he connects with Keenum, expect that to continue this week. Fitzgerald has a good matchup vs the Redskins, when he has a good match up he has the potential to go big. 
TE’s- Graham has a shocker last week, he should come back stronger this week despite the tough matchup vs LA. However there is very little comparison with what Kay has in his locker. Kay boasts the no.1 TE in the league on current form in Travis Kelce, he goes in to this week with a strong matchup vs the chargers (it is starting to look like KC vs Chargers will decide this matchup). If he goes big, he can win Kay the match up. Watch this space. 
Kickers- Succop has served Sanford well this season and against the 49ers there could be ample opportunities. However Greg the Leg is far and away the Kicker to play, he should get in to double figures for Kay again this week. 
Defence- Jags all day every day. They are so strong and constantly post big numbers regardless of matchup, Kay is once again playing the matchup against the Jets, and who knows after mcCowan had such a poor week last week, he expects more interceptions. 
The experts have a Kay victory on the cards. The Shprutzrangers are the regular season champs, their fans will hope they can bring their regular season form in to the playoffs. If they come alive, expect a big score.

---

title: 2017 · Semi-Plancey preview (part 2)
text:
*Sent 14/12/2017*

*Levey vs Leavemyarsalona* 
What we have here is a matchup of two non-believers. Prior to the first season of the Plancey, freedman seemed happy to make up the numbers, auto drafting his team and was found asking, “hang on...does this mean we have to sort out our line up every week....allowwww!” however, freedman now eats sleeps and breathes fantasy football, plotting and planning his way to superstardom. Levey was of the same ilk, it isnt necessarily that he didnt want to get involved it was more fear of the unknown. He had heard of the time and effort required to perform in fantasy football and given that his phone is often broken or sitting in the washing machine, one start button away from being broken, he feared this world wasnt for him. It is fair to say that they are both believers now and to get that coveted Plancey place you will need all the belief you can muster. 
If you didn’t see it on the news, the North Korean streets were lined with Levites after the magic of Sunday night. It was widely reported that whilst Hopkins was the star the show, it was the brilliance of Kim Jong-Un, plugged in as QB, TE and Defence for Levey last week,that stole the show and won the game for them. The highlight being the final play of the game where Kim Jong-Un threw a 90 yard pass towards the end zone, only for Kim Jong Un to run the 90 yards in 3.4 seconds to comfortably catch the pass in the end zone. Who are we to argue with that. 
The miracle Levey has performed to get to this point will go down in fantasy folklore. At the start of the season some said Levey was an amateur and didn’t know what he was doing. He is the only player not to make a single trade during the season. However, given his playoff run, his tactics have now been marveled throughout the world. The tactic has been coined the “comfort in your team” strategy and it has been rumored Levey has written a book with the same title, coming to your nearest Waterstones in the new year. There is talk that Josh Gaon has pre ordered 50 copies. 
Levey will look to continue to perform miracles this week against the favourite for the Plancey, Saul Freedman, we caught up with some Levites this week to see how they are feeling, “This is madness! The playoffs were bonus enough in our first season with a new owner. The Semi- Plancey’s too! Whats next…The Plancey?! We thought Levey’s reputation for being indecisive would affect his ability to manage this team, but it has been to our advantage! We all thought having retired players on our roster was just a sign of someone not knowing what he was doing. Scap that! Its clear now that this was all mind games. We love you Benjy Levey, sign up for life!”. 
Benjy Levey will go in to this game with absolutely no pressure whatsoever, however, for Saul Freedman it is lose or bust…..
It is crunch time for Leavemyarsalona as they suit up for the Semi-Plancey in which they may well believe this is their moment to take home the coveted prize. Freedman has been hurt before in the playoffs in the shape of Leveon Bell, but we are pretty sure he is over that now….he doesn’t really talk about it much….its not like he would put a clause in a trade to ensure he never sees him again…he isn’t really superstitious like that…..
Freedman comes in to the playoffs on the back of a strong 9-4 win loss record in the league. That makes them one of two teams in the playoffs who finished the regular season with winning records. The jury is still out on what it says about the league itself, but it made for some fantastic viewing. 
As stated in the previews last week, Saul has built his team in his image and have turned themselves in to the leagues marmite….He has been hit with criticism from all angles but when it comes to fantasy football it is getting that W at the end of the week which is all that matters. In his trade with Josh Gaon, Saul managed to pick up D Freeman, who, it has been widely speculated, is actually freedmans father. When entering the NFL D freedman missed the “D” off his surname accidentally when registering and the name has stuck ever since. It was a beautiful thing to see the family back together. However it has caused problems this week in the camp as the father and son duo have been papped for all the wrong reasons. Freedman is well known to have a strong relationship with his team, that is why his philosophy is so engrained in their minds. In last seasons playoffs, Freedman had his boys on a strict regime to keep up fitness and concentration. In an attempt to exercise the demons of last year, Freedman has removed the curfew and has taken his boys back to his old stomping ground of Birmingham for a week of drinking and partying in an attempt to calm the playoff nerves. Unfortunately on a night out at PRYZM, the freedman’s were pictured performing the infamous dentist chair drinking game. Being Saul, he has refused to apologize to the NFL stating that it is all part of the process to ensure his team are in a winning mentality come Thursday night. There have been other rumblings of members of leavemyarsalona causing problems in Birmingham this past week, Keenan Allen was fined for pissing on a homeless person outside G casino, with McCoy and Green caught in a drug fueled orgy with Birmingham’s own granny hookers.   
A bold tactic by Freedman and we will have to wait and see if his boys come in to the week feeling relaxed and refreshed in order to erase all memories of last season, which Freedman is clearly over by the way. 
Freedman, has built himself a strong team, he has had very few bumps on the road to the playoffs and getting himself in to the Plancey and then going on to win it would cap off an almost perfect season. 
To the match preview…..

QB- Lev has put his faith back in to Dak and will most likely continue to do this. He has his bumps through the season, but performed back to his best last week. Saul however, had a nightmare bye week. Having picked up the no 2 QB this season in the trade with Gaon, Wentz only went and got himself injured. Freedman has been quoted saying that there was a complete miscommunication in Wentz’s first team meeting, he was told to take it easy for week 14. Whilst a big loss to an already strong lineup, Keenum will seamlessly come in with a strong matchup vs the bengals. 
RB’s- McCoy has a strong match up vs Miami and will likely score well this week, Latavius Murray has been a bench player for the majority of the season, however, he has breakout ability given the amount action he sees. Devonta Freedman, back from injury, is also expected to post big numbers for his son this week.  
Levey at the moment is looking to play Gore against his previous employers, which is a possible story in the making. Miller will have a tougher time against the Jaguars D (which given his Houston dependence may cause him issues this week). Lynch has an interesting match up vs Dallas and given his strong fantasy pointage in recent weeks could prove strong for Levey. 
WR’s – As stated last week Jones and Hopkins have the potential to go big, last week it was Hopkins, but if I was going to predict, this week it will most likely be Jones, what a matchup he has against the Bucs. He has proven he can go big and against Leavemyarsalona he is going to need it. 
Green and Allen provide Saul with excellent options in the WR roles, both have difficult matchups but with these guys it is often not the matchup but simply the mood they are in. 
TE’s – Gronk vs Cook seems like a no contest, although Cook has performed recently for Levey so you never know. Gronk is back from his suspension and will look to make waves for his new head coach. 
Kickers- Once again who the fuck knows. 
Def- The broncos have been strong recently and Levey will need another strong performance vs a poor Colts team. Saul is currently playing without a Defence and in the words of pepper brooks, “it’s a bold strategy cotton, lets see if it works out for them” (since the writing of this he has inputted the Detroit lions in to his team, but how can I possibly take out a fantastic dodgeball joke). 
The stakes are huge, Levey has already shaken the league to its core, can the Levites pull them through once again to play in the match where legends are born. 

To finish off we have a message from the almighty Rabbi Alan Plancey who wishes you all a hearty hearty mazel tov on getting this far…..

---

title: 2017 · PLANCEY BOWL preview — Leavemyarsalona vs Shprutzrangers
text:
*Sent 21/12/2017*

The hours, the days of chopping and changing, trading and sticking, all lead up to this. Gentlemen, welcome to the Plancey Bowl 2017. The second Plancey bowl in history. The game where mere men have the opportunity to become immortal. And it all begins this thursday night. The season has had its twists and its turns, as the Borehamwood boys become more confident in their fantasy decisions. We saw trading like we have never seen before (Thanks Gaon). We saw a final weekend the likes of which we may never see again. But all of the memories and the highlights will take a back seat as one man will look to take undisputed ownership of the Plancey belt and put himself in to the hall of fame as a legend in his own time. 
Last season saw Livingston take the belt in a well fought contest. However, after losing in the first round of the playoffs he will now be handing the belt over to the eventual winner of this sloggfest. 
It seems only right that the only two teams with winning records fight it out for the plancey. There were rumblings of possible shocks from Levey and Special K, but it was not to be as Saul confirmed his squads strength going in to the playoffs and Sanford reminded the rest of the league why he was regular season champ quashing rumours that it would all fall apart early on post regseason. For Levey, the team were stretched as he was somewhat destroyed. However, it was reported in North Korea, that whilst Kim Jong-Un had a relatively quiet game of 8 TD passes and 3 rushing TD’s Levey made short work of Leavemyarsalona and look forward to their first plancey appearance. Kay also had a poor week by his recent standards however, that difference in class will show at this point of the season, as even a quiet night by Sanford’s standards was enough for the W. 

We caught up with Sanford earlier this week in the plancey press conference, "its an amazing feeling to be in the plancey, when you get to these points in your life you need to take a step back to thank all the people in your life who have got you to this point  Thanks to my family, my fiance and all the players in the shprutzrangers. But most of all i want to the thank the lads at mclagan for all the hard work on this fantasy team this year. Jason on trading decisions, mark on scouting and development, david on weekly lineup decisiions, jeremy on waiver wire picks, kevin on draft planning and strategy and paul on waiver research. Without all of you we couldn’t have got this far”.

We caught up with Sanford’s  fiancé, “is this week really the final week? I can’t believe it, he is actually going to have to talk about something else with me now. If he thinks he is bringing this belt to our apartment he has another thing coming”. 

Sanford has without a shadow of a doubt been the best this season. Consistently scoring above 100 points. Sanford had previously booked to take the squad to birmingham for the week leading up to the plancey, however, all football teams have been banned from the City after Freedman and his boys trip in the previous week. Sanford said, "i wanted to take the lads to the City where i performed my best work....if you know what i mean. However, in what can only be classed as pitiful mind games by leavemyarsalona, we have been banned. It will have to be bournemouth, i spent many a weekend there in my youth at the big family weekends away. Its just a shame the ambassadors isnt still open, I had told Mark Ingram about the dolphin in the pool and he was so excited we were going to bournemouth to see it. I didnt have the guts to tell him it had closed down years ago. 
The shprutzrangers were nothing but charming on their trip away visiting many an old age home and the beaches, sigining autographs for local fans. Sanford has stated that his boys are raring to go and will do all it takes to get the belt and take it to NY. Few teams have caused sanford problems this season, he will face his toughest test to date vs a very strong leavemyarsalona team that is coming off a 140+ week.

Freedman has got himself to a plancey and he couldn’t be in better shape for it. His team are firing on all cylinders and should fear absolutely no one.
In his plancey presser Freedman said of his opponent, “If all his players have as Low a centre of gravity as he does, we are screwed”...homage to the famous sanford backside. He added, “we have heard rumours that this is the only coach in fantasy football where the manager has the cheerleaders on speed dial instead of the players”. Clearly trying to get inside the head of the old poor performing Sanford of last year. 
After the debacle of Birmingham, we are expecting fines and suspensions from the league however, none will come in to effect until next season. We also await to hear if Freedman will have a touchline ban imposed after he ran on to the field to celebrate his father scoring a touchdown. In true Gazza style they performed the dentist chair celebration, there were yellow flags flying all over the place it was a sight to see. 
Freedman has found a formula that works after last season and he is sticking to it. Therefore, the squad have been in Magaluf this week preparing themselves for the plancey. It seems the lads have been at it again with McCoy and Murray papped bathing in their own vomit outside BCM. 
The regular season was built on scoring just enough points to win matches. Fans loved the wins but hated the style of play. Freedman seemed to be not too bothered by all the fuss. In his view it’s a results based industry. But he had a trick up his sleeve in the shape of Josh Gaon. In one double week based trade (its gaon, it had to be a staggered trade) Freedman has turned his team in to monsters. Since the trade they have scored no less than 120 points a week and will take some beating. 
It will be hard to see past Freedman in this one and we believe the belt is likely to stay in the UK. 

Given its Plancey week we thought we would catch up with the other fantasy players to see what the colleagues of the plancey managers thought of them. 

Jamie Kay has been quoted saying, “I would like to see the smug grin wiped off the face of saul!” He then added, “Gaon’s suicide trade has gifted the league to him. Can we blame it all on Gaons reckless addiction”. Clearly taking issue with saul being favourite, he didn’t actually have anything to say about sanford. What is clear though is that he is a Shprutzranger this weekend. 

Sacofsky has continued this trend. Having been beaten my saul by very small margins in both legs of the regular season matches, you can bet he is angry. He was a victim of a freedman special and has stated in regard to Saul’s team, “my mother always told me that if you don’t have anything nice to say then don’t say anything at all. All I will say is that I’ll be coming for him next season”. Clearly another incensed opponent that was so upset at Saul’s place in the plancey bowl that he also forgot to mention anything about Sanford. 

Livingston decided to talk about Saul’s state of mind in the lead up to the plancey bowl, “he wants this so bad. A win will lead to an ejaculation height to beat his infamous “that took me by surprise incident””. 
On sanford, “he wants the win but deep down he will know he didn’t deserve it. His secret guy is akin to a Russian athlete in the Winter Olympics. He knows he is the best but he didn’t win on a level playing field”. Clearly the feeling of this newspaper is felt throughout the league, although, we feel it was more of a team effort than a single guy. One day we will find out the truth. 
Livingston also pointed out, “Credit to him he used the loan gate incident in the dressing room. He’s managed to create a fergie-like us against the world atmosphere with that”. 

When discussing Sanford, Josh gaon felt a recent press conference quote by Sanford was enough, “now that I am engaged I can’t just follow my D wherever I go...that is before I met the jags. I’ll be riding that D all the way to the plancey”. Nuff said. 
On saul, “I would like to wish my offcuts good luck for the final. It feels a little like how arsenal felt when Van Persie won the league for United”. 
On the overall plancey, “the matchup between the toughest/most frustrating negotiator in the league and the most kind/ oddest negotiator in the league. It almost makes you think that being able to trade isn’t a skill you need to make the plancey”...says it all really. 

Benjy Levey was called for comment, however, his management have sent a comment saying, “no quote from the Levey manager as he is being held in captivity by the supreme leader”. That certainly doesn’t sound promising. We delved further in to the story and we have been told that what happened was that despite the loss, kim jong-un sent for Levey to come to North Korea to celebrate a great season. However, letters were sent, phones were called and doorbells were rung and nobody could find Levey. After a couple of days of this, Levey then called back, but on a withheld number which only helped with the supreme leaders paranoia that Levey was making a run for it before the next season. Therefore, we understand Levey has been imprisoned in North Korea until further notice on charges of being impossible to get hold of. 

Daniel Polak was called and had this to say, “Don’t even talk to be about Sanford, what he did over the Ertz deal will live long in the memory. He has had a strong season overall and I know how badly he wants that belt”. 
“Saul has quietly gone about his business. He has built a great side and they are going to be difficult to beat. For someone who would have done anything for a few extra wins, I admire the way his team have got him over the line time and again. A great team and a great coach, I can’t understand all these off the field antics...he needs to get that sorted for next season”.

Now for the team previews...

QB’s - Sanford has gone for Big Ben in recent weeks, but we expect smith vs Miami is the best in terms of match ups. Smith’s work has got sanford here surely that gets him a starting berth in the plancey. No such decisions to make for freedman Keenum will be looking to continue his strong form in a good match up vs GB. 

RB’s - oh what sanford could have had...zeke is back from suspension this week and should have slotted straight back in to Sanford’s team. However it wasn’t to be and now he will have Ingram and Riddick with good matchups with Mixon in the RB 3 role. However this is, on paper, no Match for McCoy, Murray and Freeman. This is an area where the plancey can be won if Saul’s boys turn up. 

WR - Landry and Hill have performed for sanford and should get a starting place although funchess is crying out for a starting place. Saul once again looks stronger on paper with Green and Allen leading the line. 

TE - gronk always wins out, but Graham has been solid for sanford since trading him in. Sanford also has an Olsen sized trick up his sleeve should he chose to play him. 

Kickers and D- on the kickers side of things who knows...but the Jags D are Sanford’s pride and joy and are playing the 49ers. If sanford has a trump card this week, this could be it. They have come through for him on many occasions this season and sanford needs one more bit of magic. 

Overall, what a great second season of fantasy football. I hope you have all enjoyed as much as I have (despite being crap...again). Thanks to the commissioner for organising. Good luck to the plancey boys, May the best team win. 

And of course Rabbi Alan Plancey wishes you both a hearty hearty mazel tov on making it this far....
