It is still the case that Polak, Lev and Sanford have not held the Plancey belt yet and they will all be looking to change that. 

We wish everyone the best of luck tonight and for the rest of the season. Hearty mazeltovs all round

---

title: 2022 · REGULAR-SEASON REVIEW / Playoff opener
text:
*Sent 17/12/2022*

The beginning of the season is always met with wild optimism. Every franchise believes it will be their year. We draft…we set our first lineups and we expect only good things…however, it doesn't take long for it all to become clear….who has the monster team, who has that one player who can get them through any gameweek and who is streaming a running back every week hoping for the best. 
Gameweeks roll in to months, losing records start to mount up and significant others become agitated by the lack of spare time in diaries. Somehow, in the blink of an eye, three months have passed and the importance of regular season records gradually fade off in to the distance. This can only mean one thing…it is playoffs time…those early season levels of optimism are refreshed with a feeling of "anything can happen" and "why not me?" For those who have made it to the party a hearty hearty mazel tov….let's take a look at this week's wild card round as our top two take a well earned rest from action…





*Lev*
It is no longer becoming a surprise to see Lev in the playoff picture. He has done it again, but his wild card entry does not tell the full story in the slightest. Having performed a full autodraft, as is tradition, Lev was laughed out of the building as it picked him 3 buffalo bills players. You could hear the taunts of "not enough diversity" and "it has picked up a player who has just been shot". The bookies had started paying out on lev to attend his first ever miriam bowl. But there was no panic from Lev…he checked his team, shrugged his shoulders and pressed optimise lineup and the rest was history. With his Bill boys leading the charge, Lev stormed to the top of the table going unbeaten through the first 5 weeks of the season and all but confirming a playoff place before anyone else could blink. Not only this but the staple signing, CmC, was not performing as expected and it was no surprise to see he was swiftly moved on to a franchise where he could succeed in his role. The NFL has declined to comment on any possible investigation in to foul play from foreign governments in relation to the trade. 
Despite this, it has not been smooth sailing for Levallo Bills, the second half of the season has not been as consistent as the first, even leading to the teeniest, tiniest possibility of a miriam bowl appearance come final weekend. Write Lev off at your peril the Plancey finalist of last season is seeking revenge for his unfortunate close loss last year and he has the backing of a nation to go and do it again. 

*Sac*
A hearty mazel tov to Sac who has made sure to let everyone who will listen know that he finally has a winning record. This is a big achievement to say the least, only trumped by his mid season Bernie award. He was kindly nominated by Josh Gaon for the "nice addition to the league" award beating out stiff competition from Liam Stein and that other Canadian guy who once joined. 
Well done on both accounts Sac….in the words of Josh Gaon, "thanks for making up the numbers". 
Sac had a tough start to the season but he used his tinkering nous in order to pivot from his team of losers to move up the league and in to playoff contention. With that out the way Sac is looking to turn his first winning record in to playoff victories to join Saul as a second time champion. Top stuff from a guy who is just nice to have around. 
As you can imagine Sac was incensed when called out by Josh Gaon on the woodcast and in a fit of rage called his agent and asked for a transfer to a league that would truly appreciate his mediocre records. Within minutes Sac had removed his pads, thrown them across the field and performed star jumps in the endzone as he removed himself from the league and set his sides on to greener pastures . Chaos ensued leading to a public apology from Josh Gaon who made it very clear that it was a miscommunication and offered Sac a taped recording of a sermon of any parasha he chooses for him to come back. Needless to say, Sac has forgiven but never forgotten and will be eyeing up a playoff match up against Josh Gaon to settle the score. 

*Saul*
Now it wouldn't be a post season without our 2 time champion. Freedman is back in the playoffs looking to make it a historic third plancey belt. 
The season hasn't necessarily gone as planned but his boys did exactly what they needed them to do and ended up sneaking in the playoffs in the last remaining spot. Some might say it would be destiny given the way the season has gone that Freedman will take his boys all the way. Needless to say, he has done it before and there is no doubt he can do it again. 
The atmosphere at Freedman HQ is not at its peak. Leading up to week 14, Freedman spoke to us and let us know that, "as long as the playoffs were made, that was all that's important". Is this a sign that the great man has given up or does he have one more trick up his sleeve. 

*Jamie*
What can be said about Jamie kay. The man looked down and out… a shoe in for miriamhood. However, he has never been there before and he made a promise that he wouldn't be there this year. He made good on his promise, all but securing his playoff status in a relegation 6 pointer with polak in week 13. By the time the final standings came around you might say Kay looked comfortably in the playoffs. 
Kay is under no illusions about this season. It has not been pretty, but having made it to the promise land Kay is in the bonus rounds and he will look to make the most of it by trying to win back belt he was forced to hand over last season. 

*Lev vs Jamie* 
This is a game coming down to the fine margins. Jamie is a man in form whilst lev is a man hoping that his stars will come good at the right time. 
It all comes down to this…the only two men in fantasy history who can boast never having had to play in a miriam bowl. One might say this is the elite club, where standards are constantly kept high. 
Whilst form generally goes out the window in the playoffs, we at the plancey post are having a tough time looking past another semi plancey for Dr Kay. 

*Saul vs Sac* 
A game involving two former champions. Both have a huge wealth of playoff experience. Much like our other match up, form would tell us that this has Sac in the semi plancey. Both have the players in their respective teams to run away with this one. It is a tough one to call but given Freedman's QB issues, at the worst possible time, the Plancey Post makes Sac favourites… 

A heart mazel tov to all and we will see you next week for the semi plancey's

---

title: 2022 · Semi-Plancey preview
text:
*Sent 22/12/2022*

Week 16 is where it's at and this week promises plenty more thrills and spills. Re-emphasising the point that when it comes to the playoffs, form means nothing, our two "out of form" players went and won their wildcard matchups. This sets us up nicely for a weekend where our bye weekers may possibly be feared like never before. A mystique surrounds our top two this year with the fans and pundits alike feeling like their respective teams may be head and shoulders above every other team, in a way we may have never seen before. But this is the playoffs and we have two underdogs licking their lips at another shot at glory in the plancey bowl. 

*Lev vs Alastair*
An apology must be made to Lev. The number of times the Plancey Post has doubted him and been proven wrong is too many to mention. We formerly apologise to Lev and the nation he so proudly represents. We predicted a loss in week 15 and it was anything but. The fans were provided a lev special as he stormed to victory leaving Kay in his wake. 
This sets Lev up for a match up with the might of Livingston. A tall ask but nothing lev has been worrying about, as you can imagine. There has been talk that he still hasn't realised he is still in the playoffs and within a chance of winning the whole thing. Be that as it may, it has been noted that for the first time in years public opinion has softened for North Korea as the world gets behind the scrappy underdog against the expected victor and goliath Livingston. 
In the lead up to the semi plancey, Lev has taken the very chilled approach. He has always believed that whatever happens happens and there isn't much more he can do about it. He takes the arm round the shoulder approach with his players which is invariably the reason he has such a fabulous record with the waiver wire. Players down in the dumps such as Watson and Jones join the brigade and results are generally instantaneous. 
Prior to the Thursday night match up of the semi plancey, Lev has decided its best for him to not be on the sidelines in order to not provide too much of a distraction to his players. He will therefore be sunning in Dubai watching via VPN as his boys look to take him to an unprecedented second straight plancey bowl appearance. 
But is this all a ruse to get inside the head of Livingston. There is nothing that will hurt more for Livingston than to have what he believes should be his, taken away from him by a player who probably won't even be watching. But, after all these years, we at the Plancey post firmly believe that Lev fully knows this and is using it to his advantage. He must beware though, it was his trip to Dubai last year that possibly lost him the chance at being the current holder of the plancey belt. 
Now let's move on to our regular season runaway leader. Livingston, for the second year in a row, has gained himself a bye week but he will be hoping for a slightly different result this year. Elly has let us know at the plancey post that she doesn't think he can take fumbling another surefire plancey victory. The world will be watching and the world will be expecting that Livingston will sweep past Lev and move on to his first Plancey bowl in the modern era, something the fan base has been craving. 
We caught up with the presenters of the award winning "Livingston lobusses" podcast this week. A podcast that follows the team across the world in the hope of a Plancey to call their own. "Surely this year is the year. It has been great to see so many wins this year. The last time we won it I was just a wee lad. My friends have always told me that it wasn't a real victory and I used to cry myself to sleep about it for years. Still do to some degree. But we can banish all demons this year". 
Livingston spent the bye week infront of his computer screens keeping a close eye on the dogs polaks team just incase their were any issues he could waste his time getting anxious about. 
However he took some time out of watching the nfl too closely to take his team on a field trip to Glasgow to rouse them up and get ready. It just so happened to be when Dallas Goedert of his beloved eagles came off IR which meant he wasn't able to fulfil his commissioner duties at the time. 
This match up has all the makings of an absolute shoot out. Lev has hit form at the right time and Livingstons teams are predicted to score 250 points weekly. This may be the semi plancey to keep your eyes on. Can Lev exorcise the demons of the past? Can Livingston exorcise the demons of the past? You have to watch this one….there are guaranteed exorcisms. 


*Saul vs Gaon* 
Freedman is back in yet another semi Plancey and he is taking it all in his stride. It is just another day in the office. He got himself to the playoffs and he let everyone know that was the season objective. But he knows more than anyone else, once your there that's where the hard work starts and Freedman does his best work in these shark infested waters. After a double dose of QB issues he calmly picked up Cousins. In the first half of his match up things were going pear shaped for Freedman as cousins was on negative points. As he was coming out for the second half Tony Romo was quoted saying that Cousins "has a Freedman sized hole in his ear". Legend has it that players have never seen a hairdryer treatment like it. Nevertheless, it worked and his second half performance propelled Freedman to a simple victory to make it to the semi planceys. 
Freedman has been known for his unconventional tactics and he was seen in attendance with his team at the Qatar world Cup final. There has been multiple rumours around the reasoning behind taking the team to this game. The first being that they needed to see what a winning team looks like, but the more disturbing murmurs from the camp are that he took the team to the see the migrant workers living conditions and made it clear that this is where they would be living if they failed to get a result this weekend. Shocking tactics if true. But hey it has worked for Lev. 
Much like Lev, Freedman is going to need all his men firing this week as he faces a mammoth task to get past Gaon's brochos. 
Josh Gaon….what can be said…is he the GOAT….is he the WOAT? All we know is that he always always brings excitement to the table…full team trades, 6 for 1 trades, season implosions, season explosions….the man cannot do anything in halves. Which bodes well for him as if all goes to plan he will be able to double his Plancey belts and try and remove the two Miriams etched in the memory of fans globally. 
Gaon has drove himself crazy this season as the closeness of the league meant there was actually a point he could have ended up back home in the Miriam bowl, despite having a career year in terms of points. Nonetheless, he won his final games to, incredibly, secure a bye and who can stop him now. 
Gaon spent his bye week working through stats that helped show he wasn't actually as bad as the world believes at fantasy football. Rumours are he was doing this in anticipation of potentially losing in the semi plancey this weekend in order to save face. Be that as it may, we all know come Thursday evening Gaon will have one thing on his mind and that is to win and get himself one step closer to the belt he feel his hard work truly deserves. Let's hope "destiny" doesn't get in his way this time.

*Plancey predictions*

At the plancey post, we are finding it hard to look past the bye week boys for this weeks match and fully expect a JK Dobbins showdown between the two in week 17. But we implore Saul and Lev to do us all a favour and prove us wrong! 

A hearty mazel tov once again to all participants and see you next week for the plancey edition!

---

title: 2022 · PLANCEY BOWL preview (semi recap 'Wow. Just wow')
text:
*Sent 28/12/2022*

Wow. Just wow. Fantasy football….what do you do to us! Semi plancey weekend lived up to the hype…and then some. Freedman had made it clear upon making the playoffs that he was just happy to be there and look where he is now…he pummelled the much fancied gaon in to submission. It was a vintage Freedman performance underlining exactly why, when it comes to the playoffs, you can never write him off. Gaon will be left wondering, yet again, what might have been, after his team, who he had told everyone who would listen, "gets about 150 a week", couldn't get to 3 figures when it mattered. 
If that wasn't big enough, the big story of the weekend has to be Lev, defying all the odds, to win a topsy-turvy matchup against heavy favourite Livingston. A victory that will go down in the ages as Lev continues in his pursuit of that coveted first belt having narrowly missed out last year. 
Both Livingston and Gaon are left to ponder what went wrong this week 17. What is becoming increasingly clear is that whilst they were both talking a big game having secured their bye week…for the second year in a row they have not been able to perform come week 16. 
For the 2 underdogs who have had to do it the hard way….we move on to the biggest game of them all…it's plancey bowl time….

*THE PLANCEY BOWL*
Its a familiar look to the plancey bowl this year as we yet again have saul vs lev as the match up. The borehamwood and Elstree choir have been brought back for the half time show brought to you by St Giles. 
Everyone thought lev's big year was last year. He had the team and he felt ready for it. After a close game, Lev agonisingly lost by half a point. The closest game in plancey bowl history. From that moment on…lev had made it his mission to get back there. As we noted, he was laughed out the building on draft night. But lev got the last laugh as his team stormed to a 5-0 start. It was at this point lev started to coast. Everyone thought the team had run out of steam. But they were just getting started…the playoffs came around and levs team started to soar… with the highlight being a victory over the beastly livingstons. Lev has peaked at the right time and who would bet against him now. 
We caught up with Lev leading up to this weeks game, "I can't believe I get my chance to avenge my loss last year. The belt is mine this year! I will be looking for any player that Saul puts on the waiver wire to bring over to my squad"...lev alluding to his genius pick up of D.J Moore to lead him to victory after Livingston had thrown him on the waiver wire. Before we headed off to our other media duties…Lev did let us know he was opening up the B.S.Levey foundation for unappreciated and unwanted talent. A beautiful gesture from a man who has made a home to many a talent who still have more to give. 


We have run out of superlatives for Freedman. The man, the myth, the legend. This will be his 4th appearance in the Plancey bowl and he is looking to be a third time champion and our first ever back to back champ. The generational talent has been lauded the world over. His stats are second to none and even when he doesn't think he has the team to do it….he manages to pull it out the bag. The sign of a true champion. This is Freedmans world and we are all just living in it. 
Despite all his QB issues he has taken it in his stride and he brushed aside a Josh Gaon who was left wanting. 
Saul never thought the Plancey bowl was realistic this year. Now that he is here, he has complete focus. Given his Lamar Jackson issues, he has been able to get the best out of Cousins and it is a love affair that has got him all the way through the playoffs. He now has that age old coaches decision to make…Cousins has got him this far, if Jackson becomes available week 17, does he stay with the hot hand?
Sources tell us that Kirk Cousins has been calling Freedman every 20 minutes just to "check in and see if he needs anything". 

*The match up*

This is going to be a tough one to call…two underdogs hitting their stride at the perfect time. They played a close one last year. Lev is definitely the fan favourite and he has revenge on his mind after last year. Freedman can go in to this game free of any pressure, just another day at the office and another shot at a belt. We don't want to predict too much especially given how poor our predictions have gone so far, we will just wish both huge luck and a hearty hearty mazel tov. 


*The miriam bowl*
How in the world are we here again. Much like the plancey bowl has the same participants, so does the dreaded miriam bowl. It has been another one of those seasons for polak and Sanford and they both look like they just want it to be over. Well luckily for them it will be after this weekend, but who will hold the dreaded title of Miriam going into next year…
Much like last year, Sanford spent the last few weeks of the regular season scrapping to get out of the miriam bowl positions and he almost did it aswell. 
The question is, having recently welcomed twin boys, will Sanfords focus be elsewhere come his biggest game of the season this weekend? The stats do not look pretty for Sanford, this is his 5th appearance in the miriam bowl (asterisk and modern era included) with a loss in this year's bowl meaning an unprecedented 4th Miriamhood (asterisk and modern era included). 
Sanford knows that despite that one magical season where he almost and probably should have won the whole thing, it has not been smooth sailing in the fantasy world.
Having spoken to Sanford earlier this week, he has not taken kindly to participating in another miriam bowl and there are rumours that the backroom staff ar facing the chop on the merry-go-round that is Sanford HQ. The objective has to be to make playoffs at a minimum next year in order to break the curse. 
For now, Sanford has a familiar foe in the shape of Daniel Polak and much like Lev, he will be looking to avenge last years loss. Rumours in the camp are that Sanford has made it explicitly clear that if anyone pulls the kind of crap that Brown pulled last year, he will be shot. 
Polak has had a very similar poor season to last year where the writing was, once again, on the wall for Polak quite early. This allowed him to make some moves in order to build for week 17. 
The miriam bowl specialist is taking part in his 4th miriam bowl and will be looking to escape for a fourth time. At some point the streak will have to end and Polak will pay for playing with fire, the questions is….is this the week? 

*The match up*
On paper Sanford has not had the opportunity to build a team for the specific week. However, he will be hoping that his studs in Hill and Cook will get him through and do enough to get past what has been a poor team in the dogs Polaks. We cannot make a call on this game but if last year was anything to go by…expect drama.

What a fantastic season…a big congratulations to the eventual Plancey. If it is Saul again….everyone else may have to give up.

---

title: 2023 · REGULAR-SEASON REVIEW / Playoff opener
text:
*Sent 14/12/2023*

Welcome back to the Plancey Post, where we have the scoop on all things playoffs. 

We must first apologise for a lack of pre season post. Our editor de cided to have a baby when we were going to print and we refused to compromise on quality. 


But here we are, plancey fantasy is back....

The offseason was marred by controversy regarding a possible Saudi takeover led my the disgruntled Josh Gaon. Whilst Golf has LIV and football has the saudi pro league, Borehamwood fantasy football has the infamous Sleeper app. Gaon has pushing hard with a supposedly huge marketing budget in order to try and push other members to join the app. In a press conference earlier this summer, Gaon was carried in via a golden chariot wearing a thobe.  He made it clear that despite fronting the Saudi sleeper league, he continues to be a staunch supporter of human rights and will continue to speak out and that the functionality of the new app was the only reason for taking the job. However, the rumoured £1mil a week salary may have also been a factor. 

Needless to say, it has caused rumblings in the fantasy community. Miriam specialist, Dan Polak, stated, “I would never take their millions just so I dont have to see when my kicker from 2010 scored a field goal.” 

The furore created a hotbed for one of the most exciting fantasy football seasons in recent years....we saw what felt like more Monday night football fantasy steals than ever, we saw Lev hold on to a kicker who didn’t actually have a real world team and we saw fireworks in week 14 leading to possibly the most exciting regular season endings we have ever witnessed. 

*SAUL (+2 planceys in NNPI)*
The GOAT is back again for another shot at the title he truly believes is his. Our only multiple world champion is still reeling after his loss last year in murky circumstances and he has stated that he has never felt more ready to get back on the horse and go for it once again.

Freedman will have to go the scenic route of the playoffs after putting himself in a spot of trouble to get to the dance. However, a week 13 win secured his playoff passage. The expectation of a third plancey belt is there as some may say he has the best team left in it.

Freedman’s seasons are often met with a controversy here and there and this year was no different as he and another trader who shall not be named (but was seen handing back all his traders anonymous badges) were involved in yet another trading scandal. Having not learnt from past issues, an attempted loan trade caused uproar across the globe. Having been chastised in the press, Freedman called a press conference and in Steve Smith like fashion uncontrollably sobbed explaining he just wanted trade innovation but never intended for it to get to this. Shame on him. 

The bookies have Freedman as the outright favourite to win it this year. 

*Ally (-1 Planceys in NNPI)*
A man for the regular season, Livingston has spent many a season heading in to the playoffs as the heavy favourite only to spunk his load too early and have his season crumble in to insignificance. But this year was different. It took an almighty MNF showdown to get him to the playoffs and there are rumblings that this is the luck he has needed in years gone by to win it all.

Since records began, amazingly, Livingston has never been involved in a plancey bowl, which has brought him in to the WOAT conversation. He will be looking to change that this year.

We caught up with Tyreek Hill who gave us some insight in to working with Livingston, “I came here to try and get that belt for coach. I am happy we got to the playoffs but it almost didn’t happen. Working under this coach is slightly weird, we do the same drills as pretty much anywhere else, but every Wednesday we have a session in the afternoon to discuss how other teams waiver wire pick ups will affect us. We also have emergency meetings that could occur at any time of the day or night to discuss how any trade in the league has affected us”. Very strange indeed. 

Needless to say, now he has made the promise land...who is to say he won’t go all the way. 

*Dan polak (0 planceys in NNPI)*
It was all about getting back to basics and cutting out the mistakes for Polak. Making playoffs with two weeks to spare was exactly what the doctor ordered. But questions have been asked about the quality of his squad and their ability to get past week 15. 

Polak has been tight lipped about his chances and has gone missing from the public eye. We at the plancey post had heard rumours that Polak had taken his team to a remote farm in the Cotswolds to train and get ready for their playoff charge.
 
We sent a team out there to see it we could interview the man they call the miriam specialist. 

It was a murky windy day as our team walked up to what can only be described as vast acres of gorgeous British greenery. We made our way through the gate marked “no trespassing”. 

After about half a km’s walk through the Cotswolds beauty, we can hear shouting and wailing noises. Making our way through the hedges, we noticed the entire dogs polaks squad standing together in a circle surrounded what must be 30 goats. In the middle of the circle, Polak was screaming at Tyler Lockett, shoving an axe in his hand, whilst holding a goat by the collar with  “Freedman” printed on its body. Polak screamed, “Do it, Tyler....we need to learn how to kill Goats! Now stop being such a pussy and kill it”. 
Tyler Lockett started to whimper, tears were streaming down his face, “I can’t do it coach”. Looking disappointed, Polak snatched the axe from Lockett’s hand shouting, “I have no time for waste men like you!” and with one fail swoop he swings the axe towards the Goat, swiping its head off in one shot. Blood squirted all over Tyler Lockett now on his knees beside himself. It was at that moment Polak noticed us in the corner of his eye. He turned towards us and paused for a second, pulled up the axe and began sprinting towards us. We had to run as fast as we could to get away and Polak bellowed after us, “tell Freedman to get his affairs in order, this weekend we slay the GOAT”. 

It is fair to say Polak may have finally lost his mind in an attempt to get the belt. 

*Sanford (-3 Planceys in NNPI)*

Mazel tov to Miriam for finally no longer being Miriam. In what has been a welcome season for sanford he has made it to the playoffs and he is absolutely delighted to say the least. There are no longer plans for a naked photoshoot this off season. 

Sanford has made it super clear that the aim was to not make it to yet another Miriam bowl appearance and now that he is here, Sanford has history of making it all the way to the plancey bowl. 

It seems the decision to take the only US franchise in the plancey league to London in the off season has worked wonders. Sanford has stated that he felt clarity on the move back and said being in the US was extremely difficult given that he had constant NFL chat everywhere he looked pushing him into making strange draft and trading decisions. 
The move to an auto draft policy has worked wonders and the question is...now the monkey is off his back can he take his boys all the way to the big stage. The man has unfinished business in the plancey bowl and he is seen as the dark horse in this year’s competition.



So here we are again, the quarter plancey and some tasty match ups. 


*Sanford vs Ally*
This is a tough one to call. Sanford never really looked in Miriam trouble and Ally came within a whisker. However when you look at the teams going in to the week there isn’t much to separate the two. Both have players capable of winning weeks on their own and sanford will be hoping his bills stack comes to play. On the other hand Ally has what feels like 2 players from every team in the league. So if particular teams do well on the week, Ally could run away with it. 
Vegas are putting Ally as favourite for the win. 


*Polak vs Freedman*
Despite the sacrifices polak has been making to the devil, this one could be over pretty quickly. Freedman could possibly feel aggrieved at having to play this match given the quality of his team. But that is fantasy, and in plancey fantasy, anyone can have their day in the sun. 
Stranger things have happened, but vegas are reporting a number of bets that Cee Dee outscores the dogs polaks this week....


A hearty mazel tov to you all and best of luck to the quarter planciers.

---

title: 2023 · Semi-Plancey preview
text:
*Sent 21/12/2023*

Welcome back to the Plancey Post where we have hit Semi Plancey week and our match ups take a familiar look

 

*Josh Gaon (-1 Plancey in NNPI index)*

Miriam positive Gaon has been walking around wearing his bye week championship belt over his shoulder in an attempt to claim an element of success in fantasy. The hope is that he gets over the fact that he got the bye week and starts to focus on actually winning a bye week game. Who better to do it against that his old foe and last weeks Miriam specialist slayer. This is the third year in a row these two will meet in the semi plancey and surely at some point Josh Gaon will win one.

Splitting his time between being the face of the Saudi backed sleeper app, being a doctor and fantasy football, Josh Gaon has been a busy man this year.

We went to Gaon’s new plush training facility to discuss how he feels about this week. As we approached the facility and there were clear remnants of what looked like a party the night before.

There was a big banner over the front of the facility reading, “Bye week champions”. Needless to say, we were met at reception by Javonte Williams, “Hi there welcome to the home of the Denver Brochos, My name is Javonte, is there anything I can help you with today?”

We were perplexed as to why Javonte Williams was the receptionist at the training facility, “Ah yes coach has decided that he has no use for me on the field and therefore, I asked for some extra work around the facility. Is there anything I can help you with?”

We asked him if it was possible to have a sit down with his coach, “Unfortunately not, Coach Gaon is actually out of the country at the moment. Off the record he has been sent to a rehabilitation facility on the west coast of the US for fantasy related addictions. A terrible shame at a crucial time.”

The league has a history of Holidays and trips abroad affecting fantasy performance and that is something that the man Gaon is playing this week knows all too well.

Gaon has had a somewhat interesting season. He would be the first man to tell you that he feels calmer and more at ease which has led to a lot less tilting and more winning. But this is where he needs his men to start performing for him, we are not sure he can take another week 16 loss. Plancey neutrality is the aim and slaying GOAT’s is the game.

 

*Jamie Kay (+1 Plancey in NNPI)*

Kay has been well and truly dominant this year in fantasy to the point that by week 6 he was planning his playoff route. Kay is starting to stake his claim for GOAT status but he knows that he needs a win this year to solidify that claim.

Kay needs to make sure his boys are not complacent going in to the bye week, especially given the recent history of bye weekers not winning the all important plancey. To keep the boys focussed on the task at hand Kay took the team to have a traditional shabbat at borehamwood and Elstree united synagogue. It gave the team the opportunity to bond and get an understanding in to where the coach has come from. Prior to shul the expectation was that the team would share out all the aliyah’s, however, when the time came, as is often the case CmC ended up just doing them all. They were provided the full experience, a financial talk from Freddy David, a request for donations from Danny Jacobs and a discussion of what it means to be the richest man in a community from Steve Davis.

As team MVP this season CmC was then stood in no man’s land between the bimah and the Aron for a world class rendition of shema Beni. Afterwards the community were invited to a kiddush sponsored by team Obi-Quon Kenobi filled with Robinson’s orange squash, that chocolate cake with the amazing brown icing with black diagonal lines on it and ready salted walkers. The team then met at 3 o clock in red road park for a spot of soccer just like old times.

Prior to the Saturday night limo tochnit, Justin Herbert and Keenan Allen were jumped as they walked past the Hart & Spool on shenley road causing injuries that have put Herbert on IR and Allen too injured to play.

Kay may need to administer some of his magic touch to get his team through given these injuries.

 

*Gaon v Saul III*

As stated this is the third week 16 game in row for these two and Gaon needs to find the answer to get one over the GOAT.

Despite being in the US, Gaon has been playing mind tricks, using his Saudi millions to pay Polak to put the dead goats head in to Freedmans bed in the week leading up to the game. The pundits have had Freedman as the best team all season, despite the slow start to playoffs, Freedman is expecting his team to come alive this weekend.

Vegas has Freedman to continue to be a bad luck charm for Gaon, but from Gaons perspective the route to plancey neutrality will require victory over the best.

 

*Ally vs Kay*

Another week 16 match up for Livingston and he will be feeling on top of the world as it was only 2 weeks ago he was a few minutes away from being in the Miriam bowl. Again, with Kay’s injuries this game may have become closer over the last couple of weeks.

Vegas has this match up even and could very much go either way.

 

A hearty mazel tov to our week 16ers and best of luck trying to get to the plancey bowl.

---

title: 2023 · PLANCEY BOWL preview
text:
*Sent 28/12/2023*

The week has finally arrived….It is the blockbuster weekend of the Plancey and Miriam bowl. Borehamwood and the world over have been waiting all year for this. For two men, the chance for legendary status awaits. One man has his sights on being the first ever 3 time winner of the belt. The other is looking to end all questions regarding the “practice” year victory. Only one man will remain standing at the end to take his place in the history books….  

In our other match up, two cousins face off in a match for the ages to ensure safety of ridicule for the next year and going forward. It is the game that nobody wants to take part in, but now they are here both are primed and ready to ensure their safety from Miriamhood. Will the cousins put their familial relationship to one side in order to succumb each other to a lifetime of pain and heartache.  

This is Plancey fantasy 2023…it doesn’t get much bigger than this…. 

 

*The Plancey bowl*

*Saul*

As the final game of the weekend came to a close, the cameras panned in on Saul Freedman. As his team took a knee to complete the customary week 16 victory over Joshua Gaon, Freedman could be seen furiously writing in his notepad and looking quite pleased with his work.  

In the post match press conference, the reasoning was quickly revealed as Freedman walked up to the podium and, with a wry smile, put his hand out to halt the reporters, “Apologies ladies and gentlemen, before I take your questions I was jotting down a few words that I wanted to get off my chest before I forget”.  

The room fell silent, with the press waiting in anticipation for a possible retirement/ loan based trade/ week 17 concession announcement.  

Freedman cleared his throat,  

“AN ODE TO GAON  

I can feel your gaze from across the way  
disappointed as another season goes astray.  

Your team bruised and battered, feeling forlorn  
In your side, I am a week 16 thorn 

Your research and analysis have brought you nothing but pain,
You’re constant bottling must be driving you insane.  

Data and figures to try and prove you are the best  
Is all we keep hearing from you– would you give it a rest?  

On pesach you may be able to eat rice,
But when it comes to week 16, I have beaten you thrice 

In your fantasy story, I may be the baddie  

But I am the GOAT, and in fantasy football, I am your Daddy!” 

 

The whole press room fell silent, and Josh Gaon could be seen at the back of the conference room staring, clearly incensed by the additional knife in the heart of this strange poem.  

Freedman looked up at the now extremely awkward crowded room and Gaon’s anger quickly turned as he could see the flop that the poetry had been.  

Then a journalist from the Miriam weekly stood up looking very serious and out of nowhere a tear fell from her right eye and she began slow clapping and began squealing, “Bravo!! Bravo!!!”.  

To Gaon’s disbelief, all of a sudden, the whole press room began providing a standing ovation to Freedman. Freedman triumphantly raised his hands as the room bellowed “GOAT!GOAT!GOAT!” 

At the back of the press room,Gaon began screaming, “ He has been lucky, every year I have built a team that I know should win it! Then this guy comes along and beats me every single time! It is infuriating!”. Out of nowhere he made a beeline for Freedman which was quickly cut at source by a couple of heavies forcefully carrying Gaon out of the room with him shrieking, “You have not heard the last of me! I will win the Plancey again!”.  

The GOAT has had to handle all sorts on his way to his third straight Plancey bowl. You heard that right! Three Plancey bowls in a row. He promised to make right what happened to him last year, and he is well on the way to making that right.  

The numbers speak for themselves and we are wondering if there are any superlatives left to describe the levels of performances we have seen over the last few years. Freedman will be looking to take his team and award winning poetry all the way to an unprecedented third Plancey belt, leaving all his rivals in his rear view mirror.  

*Alastair Livingston* 

What a season for Alastair Livingston.
A man who was so close to a Miriam Bowl and all was lost. He was left wondering if he would ever get a chance for the belt ever again. But here he is with his ticket to the dance in hand. If you had spoken to him a couple of weeks ago, he was just happy to be in the playoffs, but look at him now. He has his team working wonders, and now he is the next man up to attempt to slay the GOAT.  

Livingston took the game to Kay in week 16 and as Kay kneeled down on the field, head in his hands, Livingston took a moment to pick him up and let him know that he fully understands the feeling of a stellar regular season coming to nothing in the end.  

The night of the victory, once it became clear that he was heading to a Plancey Bowl, was party time at Livingston HQ. Bed time was extended to 8.15pm as opposed to the usual 8, with Livingston pictured snorting a line of sea salt. It was a wild night with the team and just prior to bed time Livingston could be seen in a delirious state rambling to himself in the corner, “Listen guys, I am going to bed but if Hill is injured I would like Hopkins in, but if Hopkins is injured put Montgomery in, but if he is injured put Orleans Darkwa in”. It was at this point he was seen being shepherded to bed by Elly as he cried out, “But what if there is an inactive whilst I am asleep, I simply cannot go to sleep”.  

Livingston most likely can’t believe his luck but lets be honest his team have come good at the perfect time and having had his phantom plancey rubbished by the Plancey members he will be working hard to get himself a Plancey he can truly and legitimately call his own.  

  

*The Miriam Bowl*

The streets have borehamwood have been waiting in anticipation for the biggest family feud since Harry and Megan left the royal family. It is red road vs Anthony road in a barnstorming cousin-off and we couldn’t be more excited. Karen and Daniella will have spent years discussing which of their boys had the upper hand over the other, this weekend definitely feels as though it will settle the debate.  

*Lev* 

Everything has gone wrong in Lev’s season- Jefferson’s injury coupled with inactivity and a lack of magic on the waiver wire that we have seen previously, have all been factors.  

Having had the 2 week break, Lev has already attended his 7th Plancey victory parade in PyongYang, and the whole of North Korea fell at the feet of the almighty Lev on another fantastic season.  

Deep down though, Lev and Kim Jong Un know that despite full control over the press and the information provided to the public, it will be hard to keep a Miram bowl loss out of the countries ears.  

In the meeting prior to heading back to the UK, the supreme leader made it clear that under no circumstances must Lev lose this match up and wipe away the clean record of no Miriam’s under the North Korean regime. His words were, “We all know that there are only 2 positions that are important in the Plancey fantasy, that is the Plancey and the Miriam, any other position is exponentially insignificant. We will keep up appearances here, now go and do your job and make this season as insignificant as possible in the Miriam bowl”.  

We must also note that Lev is under investigation for failure to put out an active team during the regular season and we suspect this one will go much like Man City’s FFP charges.  

Lev could now join an unwanted list of doing the ‘ole Plancey to Miriam.  

Lev is determined to win this match up and whilst he has to give back his belt sitting proudly in his lounge, he wants to make sure he doesn’t do it by taking on the fateful Miriam title in its place.  

*Sac*

Sac is also in unusual circumstances, his seasons have generally had a predictability to them. That predictability usually results in a trip to the playoffs and sometimes even more. But this season, in his own words, has been dire. Despite all that, Sac usually does just enough and as we have previously chronicled, almost made it through at the expense of Alastair Livingston, which would have resulted in an almightily different outlook given the fact that Livingston now has a place in the Plancey bowl and based on current scores, Sac actually may have made it there himself.  

Needless to say, in the history books, it will simply note that Sac is playing in the Miriam bowl, and he is hoping that it won’t state a loss.  

Sac has had an interesting season whereby some might say he has taken the Gaon mantel for being tradesmen of the year (based on volume, not quality). The team has come under fire recently, being the only Israeli based team with crowds across the world, taking to the streets to protest against Sac and his team. Demands were made of the Plancey Fantasy Football Association to remove Sac and his franchise. Gaon was front and centre of the protests quoted saying, “Look its been nice having him around, he is just there every year with his same old record, but I have talked internally to my team and I think given the situation in Israel, we can’t possibly have him involved.”  

Clearly those sleeper Saudi Millions have caused a switch of sides. Regardless, the Association made it clear that Sac has an absolute right to defend himself, be it in the regular season, the Plancey bowl or the Miriam Bowl.   

That is what Sac is hoping to do because in this cousin-off someone is going to be Miriam for the first time.  

 

A note from Rabbi Plancey, “I wish you all a hearty mazel tov on another fantastic Plancey fantasy season. Best of luck for those in the Plancey bowl and for the loser in the Miriam bowl, there is a nice big space for you in my bed!”

---

title: 2024 · PRESEASON PREVIEW
text:
*Sent 02/09/2024*

The freedman victory tour is on its third run. Are we bored yet....absolutely not...because it’s the start of the fantasy season and just like arsenal, everyone is in the false sense of belief that this can be their year. 
So what has everyone been up to....
Freedman has been revelling in yet another victory. It is believed that he was so excited by victory that he even proposed to his now fiancé. Freedman has spent the off season looking at other ways to motivate himself. “Fantasy is becoming too easy for me. There is no effort or strain to win anymore and I have found it has become quite dull. Instead of player scouting and research, I have found a new hobby watching dwarf porn in the off season. I have found it to be extremely relaxing and there are now so many genres that I have been able to rack up thousands of hours without getting bored”. It seems that being at the very top is not all it’s cracked up to be, a challenge is quite clearly needed for Freedman. It has also been requested that his new found off season hobby is kept from Hillary. 
Alastair Livingston has spent his off season working on his trading skills. After years of being laughed out of the building for his valuations, he spent some time on a course to help him get those deals over the line. After months of reading, role playing and developing his skills he felt like he would get some good practice in after the Olympics. Holding a strong potential drafting position he decided he could use it to his advantage. He felt confident that he could get it right this time. He cast his mind back to all his training and offered Polak his pick....when asked what he wanted for his position in the draft...Livingston felt a sudden fuzziness in his vision, his head began pounding....he tried to resist temptation and regain his composure, remember all he had learnt and offer something fair that worked for both sides of the deal....but the pain was too much to bear and he couldn’t hold it in any longer . The anguish was finally relieved by a simple message ” YOUR SOUL!”. 
Jamie Kay has spent his off season looking after his new baby. We caught up with him during the off season, “it’s been great having a baby. All that time taking care of a new born really gives you perspective in life. And I feel it is about time I win that belt again. Nothing has been right since I relinquished it and i must get it back”. Kay has been on a campaign in the press letting everyone know that if it were not for Freedman, he would be considered the GOAT. Now it is time to remind everyone why he is in that conversation. 
Josh Gaon comes off another semi plancey loss which has left him plancey negative once again. He was devastated as he was hoping to be plancey neutral for the arrival of his new baby. A lover of stats, we have unconfirmed reports that upon the arrival of his new baby, Gaon made it clear to the hospital and other families that his baby was statistically the best baby to have been born that day and whether they want to give him an award for super sperms or not he will forever claim to have the greatest of all time. 
Sanford has had a wonderful off season being Miriam free. The move back to London all settled he will now be looking to rekindle that one season where someone else (allegedly) was setting his team for him. He now has something to build on having dodged miriamhood for the first time in what feels like a lifetime. We expect big things this season. 
Sac has had a baby within 24 hours of the draft just like I did last year. If my season last year is anything to go by....enjoy a mediocre season Sac. As the leagues sole Israeli representative we commend him for drafting this evening amidst war and childbirth. Sac has a specific way of playing that usually results in a playoff berth and is more of a....Hey we are here, so why not us? Energy. We expect much of the same and a hearty mazel tov once again. 
Polak has spent the off season serving a prison sentence for cruelty to animals on the back of his Goat slaying incident last year with Tyler lockett. He is out now and is a changed man, but chasing a Plancey can do crazy things to you. Polak believes that this could be the year. 
Miriam Levey....how did it come to this. He has joined the list if champions to miriams and north Korea were not happy at all. Last years stud justin jefferson had to flee his summer holiday to North Korea due to a death sentence after his injury last year. Let’s hope autodraft can do him better this year.
A hearty mazel tov to everyone <This message was edited>

---

title: 2024 · REGULAR-SEASON REVIEW / Playoff opener ('One belt to rule them all')
text:
*Sent 12/12/2024*

*One belt to rule them all….*

The fantasy playoffs have come around again and the questions all the fans want to know….just like the KC chiefs...is anyone good enough to topple the three time champion Saul Freedman. The regular season has been an interesting one: Even the North Korean propaganda machine can’t find anything to shout about regarding lev’s abysmal season as he became the first man to go 14-0 in the regular season. Sanford HQ is reeling after their franchise is taking another trip to the Miriam bowl. Despite numerous Miriam bowl losses, losing to the 14-0 Lev may be the worst of them all if it was to happen. Given the state of the bottom two teams this season, the playoff picture was sewn up quite early this season, but now the drama looks set to start. We begin our plancey post playoff picture with the four men looking to battle it out for a place in the semi plancey’s.

 

*Josh Gaon – Plancey negative (-1)/ Miriam Positive (+1)*

There was a fear that the September trip to South Africa would leave Dr Gaon suffering from the fantasy curse that even derailed Saul Freedman’s season a while back. But surprisingly, Dr Gaon went on a great winning run to put himself out there as an early pace setter for the league. In fact upon return from South Africa, Gaon was so sure of himself and his team that every patient at his practice would leave with a, “I’m having a 16-0 day” sticker. As is generally the case with Dr Gaon, it is the over excitement that gets you and one loss turned in to more losses and Gaon was left staring at a mediocre regular season wondering what stat he could look at to make it all look good. Needless to say, there was no such fear that he would be in Miriam bowl contention but knowing Gaon he would have hoped for more from this season.

We went to meet with Gaon in a secret Saudi owned facility, with state of the art training equipment and conference rooms. “How do you like my new space? It came with my new Sleeper job... it has been quite lucrative…” We ask him how he feels about his season and where it is going, “Well to be honest I was hoping for more in the regular season but I think my guys have solid playoff match ups… in fact, if I could change my teams name they would be called “Sea of green””.

As we were walking and talking we go past “THE STATS OFFICE” and we ask a little about it, “I am so glad you asked, come lets take a look…” We walk in to what looks akin to a trading floor with over 100 people with 8 monitors infront of them. “This is the stats room where I have staff using every ounce of their brain power to find stats to show that I am in fact the greatest fantasy player of all time”.

A staff member runs up to Gaon at that moment, handing him a piece of paper. Gaon turns to us, “Ah would you look at that, do you know who has the most cumulative points over the time of the plancey league for their 2nd bench spot….that’s right….this guy right here….I really am just the best”…..

 

*Daniel Polak – Plancey Neutral*

Daniel Polak has spent all year dreaming of being back in this position to finally get a victory in the playoffs after some time without one. Last year he faced eventual and perpetual winner Saul freedman where he was charged after the Tyler Lockett goatgate. After taking the offseason to collect his thoughts, he has gone again with the hope of finally getting hold of the belt that matters more than life itself. We went to visit Polak at the local spa retreat after his decision to give the players a full weeks rest prior to the week 15 match up. We are met at reception by a serene looking Polak in his bathrobe, “Please come through, make yourself comfortable, it is only me and my staff here”. After getting in to more comfortable spa attire we are asked to meet Polak in the jacuzzi.

“Please come, relax, this is a full on relaxation zone. In fact you are lucky I allowed you in. I have banned all talk of football for the time I am here. But for the plancey post, I would do almost anything. Do you want anything to eat or drink….come let’s order something…..TYLER!!!!”…out of the steamy corridor comes a sheepish Tyler Lockett in an Apron, “Yes Master, how can I be of service?”

Polak glances towards us, “Ah yes, my boy Tyler hasn’t quite been the same since the old incident this time last year…He is staying by my side whilst we can rehabilitate him back to his old self…now, what would you like to eat or drink…Tyler will gladly take your order”. Tyler is visibly shaking at this point as he looks at us doe eyed. We explain that we do not want anything to eat. Polak then asks us whether we want to get in to the jacuzzi with him, “Please guys you look so uncomfortable!” When we turn down the offer Polak climbs down, “Fair enough, no worries, at least put your feet up….TYLER!” In an instant Tyler Lockett pull off a quick spin move and dives down at our feet, arching his back to allow us to put our feet up on his bent over body. When we again refuse the offer Polaks slams his hands down and bellows, “You see what you have done Tyler! You have scared them, you fool! Get out of here before I make an example out of you!” Lockett looking noticeably upset scurries away through the steamy spa and out of sight.

Polak continues as if nothing had happened, “I really believe I have an opportunity this year. We have built a strong outfit and I feel I have a point to prove this year”.

It was only once we left the premises that we informed the authorities of Tyler Lockett’s whereabouts and condition and he has since been found and Polak is currently under investigation, he has declined to comment.

 

*Jamie Kay – Plancey Positive (+1)*

They say that this game can send you wild and make you question your sanity and this sentiment is true for our next playoff contender. Jamie kay has spend some time in a rehabilitation facility to give his mind a break. The reason for this stint was due to what occurred one fateful night in the Kay household.

The rumours are that Nat could hear weeping in the middle of the night coming from downstairs and noticed Jamie was no longer asleep next to her. Nat crept downstairs to ensure she didn’t wake the baby not sure what she was going to find. The scene she was met with remains engrained in her mind to this day. There was writing all over the wall in what can only be described as Kay’s blood and faeces “272 attempts, 1459 yards, 67 receptions, 564 yards, 14 rushing touchdowns, 7 receiving touchdowns”

As Nat turned in to the lounge she was met with a naked Jamie rocking backwards and forwards in his lounge mumbling to himself and repeating over and over, “272 attempts, 1459 yards, 67 receptions, 564 yards, 14 rushing touchdowns, 7 receiving touchdowns”.

“Jamie?” Nat inquires, but he continues to rock back and forward mumbling…..“272 attempts, 1459 yards, 67 receptions, 564 yards, 14 rushing touchdowns, 7 receiving touchdowns”.

“JAMIE” she shouts, causing Jamie to stop and turn his head towards her. He looks at her and begins sobbing and howling, “They said he was going to be ok! They said he was coming back stronger than ever”.

We are glad that Jamie has got the help he needs as he looks to solidify his place as one of the greats. We can confirm he has stopped mumbling Cmc’s 2023 stats. We went to visit him at his facility and he told us, “I cannot understand all this GOAT talk regarding Saul. I should be right up there with him. I have consistently made playoffs and finals, won a plancey…it should be me!”

 

*Sac – Plancey Positive (+1)*

Sac has had so many things to contend with this season, given the War and Childbirth, he has stated that is lovely to be able to have the stability of knowing that he will finish the regular season with a record of 7-7.

Sac has always represented Israel in the group with pride and has often be treated like Israel throughout the world. This was never more the case than when our very own commissioner made his feelings on a podcast very clear about Sac. Nevertheless, Sac will always be an ever present member proving that mediocrity in the regular season does not stop you from a shot at winning it all.

Sac has become more and more vocal regarding injustice in the league and has found a partner in crime in Dan Polak as they look to argue the case for the “little guy” against the more influential franchises in the league. We went to see Sac as he heads in to another playoff run, “I know some people have made their feelings clear regarding my participation in the league, but my boys and I stick two fingers up at them and show them my plancey positive card”.

We sat down with Amon Ra St Brown to discuss what life was like under Sac’s rule, “It is quite an interesting dynamic in the regular season. Every Thursday evening Sac gets us together and he points up at a framed 7-7 he has plastered above the whiteboard and says to us….go out and try your hardest….but not too hard….but lets win…but sometimes not…It is mighty confusing. And now we have been asked to go and win 3 games in a row which is quite a tough thing to achieve when the team has built its identity on winning half our games.”

Sac heard of the quotes from his star man and said, “Look if he can’t take the heat he needs to get out the kitchen. This is the playoffs, the regular season is for losers this is where champions are made…this is where we you achieve plancey positivity….I have no time for those who find my methods confusing….it is now win or go home and against Gaon this week I am going to show him why its nice to have me in the league”.

 

*Kay vs Polak*

This is a possible revenge game for Polak as he looks to avenge the Kamara bloodbath of Kay’s Plancey winning year. This is the playoffs and despite Kays loss of Cmc he still has a team that can do serious damage.

 

*Sac vs Gaon*

Whilst it may be “nice” to have Sac as part of the group he will be pulling no punches against Gaon this week as both battle it out to try and solidify a place in the semi planceys. Saul freedman has stated that he may have another poem ready for Gaon if he can make it through this week.

 

A hearty mazel tov to all

---

title: 2024 · Semi-Plancey preview
text:
*Sent 20/12/2024*

One step away from the lights of the plancey bowl…it is time for the semi planceys with some blockbuster match ups coming our way this weekend. We welcome our bye weekers to the playoffs after a nice week off and we also had a first ever “pick who you play” scenario.

 

*Saul Freedman BSc GOAT 3x Plancey Positive*

Saul freedman has been taking time off in the bye week to rest and recuperate as he bids for an unprecedented 4th Plancey. He knows, more than ever he has a target on his back and he has come back this season winning the regular season league and looking almost stronger than ever.

Known for throwing wild parties and treating his team, Freedman has taken the team back to Ibiza to get ready for the playoffs and was pictured drunk on stage with David Guetta showering the adoring fans with champagne.

We went out to Ibiza to have a chat with him and were asked to join him at the O Beach Pool party where Saul was sitting in a luxury canopy next to Sam Darnold and Taysom Hill (with his knee in a brace) having a shisha looking as relaxed as can be. Saul gives us a knowing look, “How do you like my boys, I pick them up off the street and infuse them with fantasy points. Before they know it they are hitting their fantasy point ceiling”.

Taysom Hill pulls himself up by his crutch and asks if anyone wants a drink from the bar. Pulling himself along he stops next to us and bends over and quietly says, “I owe everything to Mr Freedman so no funny business with your questions today”.

We look over to the left of the tent and there are 4 bikini clad women, 3 of them holding replica plancey belts. Freedman notices our stares and explains, “Ah yes these lovely ladies follow me everywhere I go holding my silverware. You may be wondering why the fourth is not holding a belt….that could be for two reasons….one, she is awaiting the 2024 belt or two, she is there as a reminder of when the whole league fucked me after the Hamlin injury and it lets me know that I must show no mercy to win as many championships as possible”.

Asked whether he feels he has any rivals this season that could take his crown, he shrugged his shoulders and said, “rivalry is not in my vocabulary. I have no rivals. My only rival is myself in past seasons”. This answer does nothing to dispel the rumours that Freedman is looking to join a new 8 man league where he takes on the managerial role of all 8 teams in a move that he has said will increase competitiveness.

 

*Alastair Livingston 1x Plancey Negative/ 1 x Miriam Positive (pre season friendly winner pre league inception)*

Alastair Livingston is in the all too familiar position as favourite for the plancey. But being favourite does not get you the belt as he knows all to well.

With his team firing on all cylinders, he has been a tough match up for all involved scoring a beastly amount of points on his way to the bye week.

We met up with Livingston to talk a little bit about his season, “It has been a great season, much better than Polak’s season it has to be said”. We pressed him further asking him what it was like to beat each player consecutively during the regular season and he responded, “It felt great, I bet Polak didn’t do that this season. Make sure you tell Polak I did that…I am invincible”. It couldn’t be clearer that Polak has not paid one solitary dollar of rent to live in Livingstons head and it is definitely getting to him. What is clear is that Livingston is in a no win situation here. Getting to the plancey bowl should be expected after his excellent regular season, but a loss to Polak would be something that he couldn’t live with.

As we continued the interview with Livingston, we could see him constantly looking at his phone, “Just checking the algorithm, in the last 2 minutes we have been talking my team’s power ranking score have gone from a 100 to 99”. We decide to quickly head for the exit as Livingston picks up a baseball bat and starts smashing every piece of furniture in sight.

 *Freedman vs Gaon*

In typical Gaon fashion, he has finally won a playoff game for some time, in the most dramatic of fashions in must be said, only to now have to face the man that has beaten him in the semi planceys three years in a row. It felt like destiny after having inputted the new rule of the top team choosing their opponent, in the hope that he would have the choice and avoid facing Freedman, but it wasn’t to be. Gaon, Freedman and semi planceys will forever be intertwined and if you believe in the law of averages, this is not the match up for you because there has only been one winner.

Freedman became the first man to get to pick his opponent for the semi planceys and despite having the choice of three, when Gaon made it through his first round there was only one man to pick. After last years Ode to Gaon, everyone is wondering what Freedman will do after this years victory.

Gaon on the other hand knew this day was coming and has set himself up early in the knowledge that he would come up against Freedman and likely lose on his way to the Plancey Bowl. But there is definitely an opportunity to get a victory, especially after a mediocre night from Sutton last night has put the tie back in the balance.

 

*Polak vs Livingston*

This is the match up the fans have been waiting for….These two have been at eachothers throats all season and now they will fight to get themselves a shot at superstardom. Two men will walk in and only one man will walk out and on to the plancey bowl.

We took a walk around Finchley with Dan Polak to discuss this weeks match up. “I am extremely excited at the prospect of this semi plancey. I have been waiting some time for it and I feel ready for whatever comes of it.” He then stops in his tracks, “ hang on just one second….how fast can you run?”

We explain that we can move a bit and Polak says, “Ok follow me”….Polak then walks us up to a front door and says, “get ready to run”. Within seconds, Polak has knocked on the door and ran a mile down the road. We follow quickly and as we look back we can see an angry Livingston gesturing towards us, “stop knocking on my door at all hours of the day Polak!!!!”

A run turns in to a brisk walk as Polak sniggers, “between Tyler Lockett and I, we have knocked and run on his door 250 times in the last week. We have a few other things planned this week including letting the Palestinian Solidarity Campaign know that Benjamin Netanyahu is secretly sleeping over at Livingstons house resulting in a planned camp site outside his door”.

It seems Polak will use everything in his arsenal to put Livingston off and it seems only fair given he is facing what fans arguably believe is the favourite to win their first ever Plancey after a dominant display in the regular season.

Livingston will hope he can finally get himself over the line after a number of heartbreaking losses after dominating the regular season. Fans have questioned whether he is the right man to get them over the line and to the promise land of planceydom.

A win against Polak this week will put many of those fears to rest.

---

title: 2025 · REGULAR-SEASON REVIEW / Playoff opener
text:
*Sent 11/12/2025*

Here we are, back at the pinnacle of sporting excellence, the Plancey playoffs 2025 and the season has not disappointed. 

The regular season has had some record performances, a score correction victory and wins by the smallest of margins.

Fantasy football has once again taken over 8 franchises (possibly 7 and a half/quarter) lives for yet another season and the question has to be whether anyone has it in them to break the dominance of the Freedman.


Before we jump in to the playoff franchises and match ups we just want to make special mention to Rabbi and Miriam Plancey who recently passed. They are the cornerstone of this fantasy football and we will make sure their memory lives on for many years to come in our own weird way. May their memories be a blessing. 

This week is the quarter plancey’s with some really exciting match ups.

*Sanford (Very Plancey negative) v Jamie (Plancey positive)*

Sanford Plc has hit this year’s targets and they hit them early. Having many a fantasy heartache, Sanford built his season on a strong foundation of 5 wins from the first 5 games. From that position it was almost an impossibility for another season in the Miriam bowl and once that box was ticked, Sanford and his team went on a rest of season long hiatus as the playoffs drew closer.

Sipping on a pina colada in Vegas, Sanford was asked by an interviewer at his sun lounger if he should not probably get back to manage the day to day operations at the team and he responded with the following, “Have you been on the sleeper app? Have you seen that green tick next to my name? my job is done here. I will be back for the post season”.

Despite being 3-6 in his last 9 games, a big confidence boosting victory vs the no1 seed to see out the regular season will leave him flying high going in to the big quarter final match up.

Sanford will be raring to go also boosted by relinquishing the Miriam name that has stuck to him like glue over recent years. Playoff berth has allowed him to move on and he will be looking to go on the long road to plancey positivity….


For years and years the Plancey post has lauded Jamie Kay and his ability to fix his teams issues to ensure he always has a part to play in the right bracket of the post season. 

This season all was not well at Kay HQ. Sitting at 1-8, nobody could quite understand how it all went wrong. Rumours of player unrest was swirling around the fantasy universe. Having been on IR for much of the season, Omarian Hampton came through the mixed zone after yet another loss and let rip on the head coach and his tactics. “This is my first season in the nfl, I went on IR and Jamie hasn’t said one word to me since. I feel completely let down and none of the boys understand any of the tactics that he is throwing out there. I can’t believe I am going to be playing in the Miriam Bowl in my first season”.


Kay had caught wind of the interview and it lit a fire under him. 
The next day as the team were sat around in the lecture theatre to watch film, Kay calmly quietens the room, “Lads, we are 1 and 8. If we want any chance of making the playoffs we have to win all our final 5 games. I need everyone in this room together in the fight otherwise we will could potentially lose the worst loss in all of sports. I am not ready to be Miriam and I don’t want that for any of you in this room”.

Kay then stops for a moment and turns to Omarian Hampton, “Hampton! Stand up please!” Looking confused, Hampton stood up. Kay continues, “Please come down here next to me”. 
Hampton, still in his walking boot carefully makes his way to the front. “As I was saying. To make it to the playoffs I need everyone with me. Standing next to me is a man who I do not want to be in the trenches with. This is a man that talks about internal information to the press. This is not a team mate, this is a man who is ready to tear us down at our weakest moment. I have been in this league over 10 years, I have taken teams from the depths before and I have even had the pleasure of holding the belt aloft in front of the world. What about you, Hampton?”.

Hampton stood there, speechless. You could feel the tension building in the room.

Kay turns and moves towards Hampton, “I am a believer in this team. You are trying to bring us down and I will not have it. Who do you think you are talking about this team”. 
You could feel the level of support building around the room for Kay. The players were beginning to believe again, nodding and murmuring along with Kay.

“I am trying to build a winning team and I have no time for people like you in this team”.
Within a split second, Kay twists his hips and round house kicked Hampton in the head, instantly knocking him out.

There was a gasp of silence as Kay stands over the unconscious running back, “You’re traded”.

The whole room erupted and from that moment on the team has gone on an unforgettable run of 5 wins in a row culminating in a dramatic victory over fellow relegation rival Sac.

This match up has everything we could ask for, a possible fading 2025 force vs a team on an absolute high from a 5 game winning streak to make it in to playoffs. Both teams have met their season objectives, one had it wrapped up early the other on the final day of the regular season. Can they now pick their teams up to go again and get themselves a place in the semi plancey’s only time will tell.
 
*Gaon (Plancey negative) vs Saul (Very Very Plancey positive)*


Plancey Negative Gaon has once again created a file of all the stats that will tell you he has had the best team this season. 
But he will need everything in his arsenal to fend off the GOAT this week in another epic match up for the ages.

Gaon has built the majority of this season on having built a WR farm whereby he held the majority of the top 15 WR’s in his stable. Unfortunately he could only play four at one time.

Gaon will do anything to ensure that he gets a win this week having had many a playoff loss to Saul Freedman. So much so he has resorted to copycat rituals that didn’t even work.

We were invited to come and visit Josh Gaon at his place in the country where the team were getting ready as he had told us he was performing the Goat slaying rituals that Polak had tried previously. He had sent us a message to let us know, “Polak had got it all wrong and that the only way these rituals work is if you have the opponent present at the ceremony”. 

Therefore we hitched a ride in a van to the country with Saul Freedman to the countryside to go to the ritual.

On the journey we were able to have a conversation with Freedman, “I love watching my opponents squirm, it is the most enjoyment I get out of fantasy having won the belt so many times. However, nothing is more enjoyable than watching Gaon squirm. I couldn’t miss this opportunity”.

We asked him about the season, “Look it hasn’t been my best season and it could be difficult to three peat but then when I look at the chumps I have to play against it gives me belief that I can do it again. Losing Nabers was unhelpful but my guys can do it on their day.”

We finally arrive at the farm where Gaon has been there waiting for us, holding and stroking a Goat in his hand.

 “Welcome friends, we have been waiting for you”, Gaon has that maniacal smile on his face that says he hasn’t slept in days and it wildly overthinking the match up.

We park up and make our way out of the van. Freedman requests Gaon gets some of his men to help him with the van. As we walk around the corner Gaon’s whole team are in a circle surrounded by what must be 30 goats.

“What do you think? Bought these all from Polak, vital for the ceremony, you see. We will start the ceremony as soon as Saul and my men get what he needs out his van.”

At that moment freedman walks around the corner, Plancey belt over his shoulder and 4 of Gaon’s men carrying a large gaming style chair. They set it down in the corner of the field and Saul sits himself down, “Had to bring my cuck chair with me”.


Gaon, looking frustrated and incensed turns away, “It is time to get going with the sacrifice”.

We began having flashbacks of Tyler Lockett slaying the goat in previous years.

Gaon walks over to the tools table and picks up the chainsaw and revs it up. “Right who will be the man to perform the sacrifice for me”. He walks around his team, who all look completely perplexed.

Gaon stops at Joe Burrow. Burrow starts shaking, “Please Josh, don’t make me do this”.
Gaon calmly says, “But Joe, it must be you. You are the one”.

Saul sniggers in the corner, in disbelief in what he is actually watching.
“Quiet you!” Gaon screeches. “Just sit and watch. Do not take your eyes off this and SHUT UP”.

Gaon turns back to Joe Burrow and hands him the chainsaw, “Right lets get a move on!”
Brown and Chase are now holding the sad Goat with Saul’s name written on it. Joe Burrow has tears streaming down his face. He starts walking with Gaon towards the poor goat and in an instant drops the chainsaw on the floor and starts running the other way.

Gaon yells, “GET HIM!”.
Justin Jefferson runs and catches up with Burrow, grabbing him. Burrow reluctantly submits.

Gaon snorts, “Well done JJ, that may your first catch since you joined this team, you cretin!”
Burrow picks up the chainsaw and assumes position. Gaon gets closer to him as he revs up the chainsaw.

Gaon whispers in his ear, “I have been waiting for you all year Joseph. You were born for this and now we must exorcize our demons before we play the GOAT this weekend if we are going to have any chance”.

Burrow nods, his face drenched in tears as he moves towards the helpless goat…..
 
A hearty mazel tov to all and looking forward to another fantastic week of quarter plancey action.

---

title: 2025 · Semi-Plancey preview
text:
*Sent 19/12/2025*

Welcome back everyone to the Plancey post where we are at the semi plancey’s stage of the competition.
 
*Saul v Ally*
Our first semi plancey takes us to a replay of last years Plancey Bowl where Livingston is trying to take on the might of Freedman to try and stop him in his tracks to threepeat.

After another playoff victory, Freedman was feeling good about his chances in the press conference, “Another weekend, another playoff victory. There is no one in this game that can stop me.”

It was at that moment Gaon burst in, gun in hand, pointing it towards Freedman. The whole room goes silent.

“Listen, nobody has to get hurt. I am in a bad place, but nobody has to get hurt”.
Gaon begins talking to Freedman, “Listen, you win, again, fair and square and destroy another season for me. But please, no silly anecdotes at my expense, no short quips about my fantasy skills and please no Ode to Gaon”.

Freedman, looking like a man who is calm in the situation but understands that he is dealing with an instability.

Freedman stands, “Look Josh, you don’t have to do this. There is still next year and you have about 30 other leagues you can try and win.”

Gaon shouts, “BUT THIS IS THE IMPORTANT ONE AND YOU KEEP RUINING IT FOR ME!”

Freedman starts to walk towards Gaon, “I know and it isn’t my intention to have so much fun at your expense. But look I promise I won’t utter one more word about you”.
Gaon starts to come to, “I appreciate that but I need guarantees. I want you to hand it over”.
“Hand what over?”
“Just hand it over so I can be sure”.

Freedman, still with the gun pointed in his direction admits defeat and puts his hand in his pocket where a folded A4 sheet of paper with the title “Ode to Gaon 2.0”. He hands it over to Gaon, who snatches it out of his hand and then frantically runs for the exit where he is met by security and law enforcement.

The press conferences and media weeks leading up to the semi plancey’s have been held in Vegas and Freedman was making his way through Caeser’s Palace casino after the press conference as he was making his way to his next TV interview where he was stopped by a cluster of green and white all shouting profanities his way.

The mob was shouting, singing and dancing at the top of their lungs about Henrik Larsson.
As Freedman tried to walk away a shirtless man fell out of the crowd and fell right in front of Freedman.

The shock on Freedman’s face when he realised it was Alastair Livingston his opponent for the week.
